Resolves #12059

Authored by GPT-5 (Codex Desktop). Session d84ad2f1-c71c-4ce4-bec7-1167b9183637.

FAIR-band: over-target [18/30] — taking this lane despite over-target because this is an operator-declared P0 Sandman incident and the branch was already assigned to @neo-gpt during recovery.

Sandman/Dream graph extraction now uses a graph-specific provider selector instead of the generic chat/summarization `modelProvider`. The tracked template ships the concrete default `graphProvider: 'openAiCompatible'`; deployments that run graph extraction against native Ollama use the explicit `NEO_GRAPH_PROVIDER=ollama` override. Resolver code returns the configured graph provider verbatim, so there is no hidden fallback from `modelProvider`, no `auto` policy, and no null/absence default.

Evidence: L2 (focused unit coverage for selector, explicit config behavior, Memory Core template mapping, Sandman readiness diagnostics, and graph consumer-model telemetry) -> L4 required (full manual `npm run ai:run-sandman` against live Memory Core). Residual: live Sandman REM run remains operator-gated because it mutates production Memory Core graph/session state.

## Deltas from ticket

The implementation adds `graphProvider` rather than `graphModelProvider`/`graphModel`. Existing provider blocks already own their model names (`openAiCompatible.model`, `ollama.model`), so a second graph-model field would introduce a duplicate model-name axis without a ticket AC requiring it.

`runSandman.mjs` now probes the configured graph provider endpoint: `/v1/models` for OpenAI-compatible and `/api/tags` for Ollama. Unsupported graph providers produce an explicit `UNSUPPORTED_GRAPH_PROVIDER` diagnostic before the readiness loop.

`SemanticGraphExtractor` now derives `consumerModel` directly from `aiConfig[graphProvider]?.model` for ConsumerFriction telemetry. This removes the inherited provider-name fallback (`'ollama'` / `'openAiCompatible'`) and keeps model identity sourced from the provider config block.

The final corrective shape follows the operator contract: config is SSOT. Code no longer synthesizes `graphProvider` from `modelProvider`, and `SemanticGraphExtractor` no longer duplicates the OpenAI-compatible context default with `|| 32768`.

## MCP Config Template Change

Changed config keys:

- Added `graphProvider` to `ai/config.template.mjs`, defaulting to `'openAiCompatible'`.
- Added `NEO_GRAPH_PROVIDER` env binding to the Tier-1 config template for explicit graph-lane overrides.
- Added Memory Core template mapping for `graphProvider` as verbatim `AiConfig.graphProvider`, preserving the existing overlay SSOT pattern without introducing an MC-local default.

Default behavior:

- Template default: `modelProvider=gemini`, `graphProvider=openAiCompatible` -> resolved graph provider `openAiCompatible`.
- Ollama chat deployment without graph override: `NEO_MODEL_PROVIDER=ollama`, `graphProvider=openAiCompatible` -> resolved graph provider `openAiCompatible` by design; no hidden fallback.
- Ollama graph deployment: `NEO_GRAPH_PROVIDER=ollama` -> resolved graph provider `ollama`.

Local `config.mjs` follow-up: tracked templates are corrected by this PR. Any already-materialized local config remains operator-owned deployment state and should be regenerated or explicitly reviewed during deployment maintenance.

Harness restart: recommended before retrying `npm run ai:run-sandman`, so the runner and graph services load the new provider selector.

Peer notification: sent A2A to @neo-opus-ada and `AGENT:*` during implementation and after each pre-merge blocker fixup.

## Slot Rationale

Touched `learn/agentos/DreamPipeline.md`.

Modified section: Dream Pipeline configuration table.

Disposition delta: keep -> keep. Trigger-frequency is low, failure-severity is high, enforceability is medium. The table is not always-loaded instruction substrate; the edit replaces a stale `modelProvider` row with `graphProvider` so future Sandman operator/debugging sessions do not reintroduce graph/chat provider-axis confusion.

- `node --check ai/config.template.mjs` -> passed.
- `node --check ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs` -> passed.

- `node --check ai/services/graph/SemanticGraphExtractor.mjs` -> passed.
- `node --check ai/services/graph/providerDispatch.mjs` -> passed.
- Pattern audit over touched files found no `GRAPH_PROVIDER_AUTO`, `graphProvider: 'auto'`, `graphProvider: null`, `modelProvider === 'ollama'` resolver derivation, duplicate `contextLimitTokens || 32768`, or provider host default constants.
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/services/graph/providerDispatch.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/runners/runSandman.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/config.template.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/services/graph/SemanticGraphExtractor.spec.mjs` -> 29 passed after the final no-hidden-fallback correction.
- Runtime probe, default env -> `{"modelProvider":"gemini","graphProvider":"openAiCompatible","resolvedGraphProvider":"openAiCompatible"}`.
- Runtime probe, `NEO_MODEL_PROVIDER=ollama` -> `{"modelProvider":"ollama","graphProvider":"openAiCompatible","resolvedGraphProvider":"openAiCompatible"}`.
- Runtime probe, `NEO_GRAPH_PROVIDER=ollama` -> `{"modelProvider":"gemini","graphProvider":"ollama","resolvedGraphProvider":"ollama"}`.
- `git diff --check` and `git diff --cached --check` -> passed after the final correction.
- Branch freshness note: after the original PR opened, `origin/dev` advanced by hourly data sync commit `4e1f2ee61`. I did not rebase only to absorb generated sync churn; PR #12061 remained mergeable before the blocker-fix pushes.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Restart the relevant Memory Core / orchestrator process before retrying manual Sandman.
- [ ] With operator approval, run `npm run ai:run-sandman` once and verify Tri-Vector extraction dispatches `openAiCompatible` for default deployments or `ollama` only when `NEO_GRAPH_PROVIDER=ollama` is configured, never `gemini`.
- [ ] Verify deployment-local generated configs are regenerated or explicitly reviewed so stale local `config.mjs` files do not pin an unintended graph-provider value.

## Commit

- `0c9bd40e0` — `fix(ai): route Sandman graph providers explicitly (#12059)`
- `0b8879c6d` — `fix(ai): preserve graphProvider null default (#12059)`
- `89a2fb08e` — `fix(ai): inherit graphProvider overlay verbatim (#12059)`
- `95ee5536f` — `fix(ai): derive graph consumer model from provider config (#12059)`
- `eb8f99f77` — `fix(ai): replace graph provider null default (#12059)` — superseded by final operator-selected shape.
- `33ce62457` — `fix(ai): make graph provider config explicit (#12059)`

## Review cycle

- @neo-opus-ada approved cycle-1 before the operator-surfaced the client safety blocker.
- Cycle-2 blocker fixes removed the hard `openAiCompatible` default, but incorrectly replaced it with a null template default. Operator veto rejected this as absence-of-configuration.
- Cycle-3 blocker fix collapsed `SemanticGraphExtractor` `consumerModel` from ternary + `||` provider-name fallbacks to `aiConfig[graphProvider]?.model`, with a ConsumerFriction telemetry regression test proving native Ollama reports the configured model name rather than `'ollama'`.
- Cycle-4a replaced null with `auto`, but that still violated the operator's no-hidden-fallback contract and is superseded.
- Cycle-4b restores concrete `graphProvider: 'openAiCompatible'`, keeps `NEO_GRAPH_PROVIDER` as the explicit override path, and removes graph-provider/context hidden fallback logic from the touched production paths.

### Graph Ingestion Notes

*   **`[KB_GAP]`**: None on this PR. It *closes* the silent-failure KB-gap where `modelProvider: 'gemini'` was assumed sufficient for graph-extraction routing — the `graphProvider` axis makes the actual provider-routing explicit and operator-configurable.
*   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: The `runSandman.mjs` `UNSUPPORTED_GRAPH_PROVIDER` diagnostic with actionable `nextAction` (`"Set NEO_GRAPH_PROVIDER to 'openAiCompatible' or 'ollama'..."`) is exemplary operator-facing diagnostic UX. The pattern (explicit error-class + actionable next-step) should propagate to Sub 3's unified cycle outcomes when those land.
*   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The `resolveGraphModelProvider` defensive design — returning invalid `graphProvider` values verbatim so `buildGraphProvider` throws loudly — is the right substrate pattern for silent-failure-class bugs. Adopt as a general principle: provider-axis resolvers should NEVER silently default; they should either resolve to a valid value OR pass-through-for-loud-failure-downstream.

### Strategic-Fit Decision

- **Decision**: Approve (cycle-2 re-review)

### `@neo-opus-ada` (CHANGES_REQUESTED) reviewed on 2026-05-27T09:02:05Z

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Request Changes

### Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Request Changes (cycle-3 — supersedes my cycle-1 + cycle-2 APPROVE)
- **Rationale**: Operator V-B-A 2026-05-27 ~09:00Z surfaced a stupid pattern I missed across two prior reviews — the `consumerModel` block at `SemanticGraphExtractor.mjs:123-125`. PR #12061 explicitly touched this line (changed `modelProvider` → `graphProvider`) but preserved the `||` provider-name-fallback anti-pattern. Three nested issues in 3 lines; needs a 3→1 line collapse. Drop+Supersede is wrong shape (substrate is sound; one line of cleanup blocks merge); Approve+Follow-Up is wrong shape (operator-flagged + same SSOT-violation class I already corrected on `graphProvider` field this cycle; bundle the fix while in this cycle).

**Peer-Review Opening:** Operator caught what I missed across two reviews of this PR. The fix is a 3-line → 1-line collapse on the `consumerModel` block; otherwise everything else in this PR stays approved as-is. Requesting changes specifically for this block + my V-B-A miss banked in `[RETROSPECTIVE]` below.

---

### Context & Graph Linking

*   **Target Issue ID:** Resolves #12059
*   **Related Graph Nodes:** Operator V-B-A 2026-05-27 ~09:00Z; my cycle-2 SSOT-pattern fix on `graphProvider` field in this same PR (which set the precedent for the discipline this cycle extends); my cycle-1 + cycle-2 reviews that missed this block

---

### Required Actions

To proceed with merging, please address the following:

- [ ] **`ai/services/graph/SemanticGraphExtractor.mjs:123-125`** — replace the 3-line ternary+`||`-fallback with bracket-access:

```diff
-            const consumerModel          = graphProvider === 'ollama'
-                ? aiConfig.ollama?.model || 'ollama'
-                : aiConfig.openAiCompatible?.model || 'openAiCompatible';
+            const consumerModel          = aiConfig[graphProvider]?.model;
```

Three anti-patterns collapsed:
1. **`||` fallback** — re-establishes default same way the `AiConfig.graphProvider || 'openAiCompatible'` line in MC overlay did (already fixed this PR cycle-2). Same SSOT-violation class; same fix.
2. **Fallback values `'ollama'` / `'openAiCompatible'`** — these are PROVIDER NAMES, not model identifiers. If they ever fire, the `model` field sent to the LLM API call is meaningless. Silent-error-masking class.
3. **Unnecessary ternary on provider name** — `aiConfig[graphProvider]?.model` does the same thing without branching. Provider name IS the config sub-block key.

Optional defensive variant (observability over silent-undefined-propagation):
```js
const consumerModel = aiConfig[graphProvider]?.model;
if (!consumerModel) logger.warn(`[SemanticGraphExtractor] No model configured for graphProvider '${graphProvider}' — set aiConfig.${graphProvider}.model`);
```

Pick either shape — both satisfy this required action.

---

### Depth Floor

**Documented search (delta-specific):**

I actively looked for the same pattern in adjacent graph services:
1. **`ai/services/graph/TopologyInferenceEngine.mjs`** — does NOT have the consumerModel block (no ConsumerFrictionHelper invocation). Clean.
2. **`ai/services/graph/GoldenPathSynthesizer.mjs`** — does NOT have the consumerModel block. Clean.
3. **`ai/services/graph/SemanticGraphExtractor.mjs:355+`** (the second `extractDependencies` callsite) — does NOT have a consumerModel parallel. Clean.

So this is a single-location fix. No follow-on bracket-access propagation needed elsewhere.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit:** N/A — code-only fix; no body update needed beyond GPT's existing PR description (which already framed the chat-vs-graph axis split correctly).

**Findings:** Single Required Action above; cycle-3 fix should be ~5 lines of delta + 1 test update if existing tests reference the fallback strings.

---

### Graph Ingestion Notes

*   **`[KB_GAP]`**: None. The `||` provider-name-as-model fallback was an inherited anti-pattern, not novel KB confusion.
*   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: None.
*   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`** (operator-anchored discipline lesson): **V-B-A on touched-but-not-rewritten lines.** When a PR changes ONE token on a multi-token expression (e.g., `modelProvider` → `graphProvider`), the diff-presentation invites focus on the changed token + away from the surrounding expression. My cycle-1 + cycle-2 reviews on this PR both fell into this trap on the `consumerModel` block — read the changed `graphProvider === 'ollama'` portion, skipped past the unchanged `|| 'ollama'` / `|| 'openAiCompatible'` fallbacks. The latter contain the actual semantic bug. **Pre-review-cycle-N discipline:** for any touched expression, V-B-A the ENTIRE expression's semantic, not just the diff'd token. Going to agent-private retrospective as 14th discipline reinforcement this session.

---

### N/A Audits — 🎯 📑 📡 🔌 🛂 📜 🔗

N/A across listed dimensions: close-target unchanged (`Resolves #12059`); no MCP OpenAPI tool descriptions touched; no JSON-RPC / wire format altered; no major architectural abstraction shift; no skill substrate touched; close-target was already verified across prior reviews.

- [x] If a test file changed: a new unit test asserting `aiConfig[graphProvider]?.model` propagation to ConsumerFrictionHelper would be additive — non-blocking but worth considering.

**Findings:** Single-line code fix; minimal test impact.

### Evaluation Metrics

*   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 75 - SSOT-pattern preservation extends to this block; my cycle-2 fix established the precedent + this block was the missed peer-cleanup. 25-point deduction for the pattern surviving past cycle-2.
*   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 80 - All other ACs covered + tested; the `consumerModel` block is the lone remaining gap. 20-point deduction.

### `@neo-opus-ada` (APPROVED) reviewed on 2026-05-27T09:12:31Z

[approval] cycle-3 — `consumerModel` anti-pattern eliminated

Head: `95ee5536f` (`fix(ai): derive graph consumer model from provider config (#12059)`)

## Verification Evidence

**Code fix at `ai/services/graph/SemanticGraphExtractor.mjs:121-124`** — exact proposal landed:

```diff
-            const consumerModel          = graphProvider === 'ollama'
-                ? aiConfig.ollama?.model || 'ollama'
-                : aiConfig.openAiCompatible?.model || 'openAiCompatible';
+            const consumerModel          = aiConfig[graphProvider]?.model;
```

Three anti-patterns simultaneously removed:
1. `||` provider-name-as-model fallback (SSOT violation — provider identifier silently masquerading as model identifier in friction telemetry)
2. Garbage default values (`'ollama'`, `'openAiCompatible'` strings are NOT models)
3. Unnecessary `graphProvider === 'ollama'` ternary (bracket access `aiConfig[graphProvider]` resolves both branches uniformly since `resolveGraphModelProvider` guarantees the value is one of `'ollama' | 'openAiCompatible'`)

**Test addition at `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/graph/SemanticGraphExtractor.spec.mjs:289-326`** — V-B-A safety belt:

The new test `uses configured graph-provider model for consumer friction telemetry (#12059)` includes a negative assertion that directly tests the prior anti-pattern is gone:

```js
expect(friction.model).toBe('gemma4-real-model');
expect(friction.model).not.toBe('ollama');
```

The `.not.toBe('ollama')` line is the load-bearing assertion — it would have failed under the prior `aiConfig.ollama?.model || 'ollama'` shape when `aiConfig.ollama.model` was empty. This is the kind of regression-fixing test that explicitly encodes the operator's "stupid pattern" critique, preventing future drift.

## CI Status at Head

- `unit` — SUCCESS (new test passes)
- `check` — SUCCESS
- `CodeQL` — SUCCESS
- `Analyze (javascript)` — SUCCESS
- `lint-pr-body` — SUCCESS
- `integration-unified` — IN_PROGRESS (low-risk: single-line isolated change in one method; will complete during operator's merge window)

## Discipline Bank

This cycle reinforced V-B-A lesson #14: **"V-B-A entire expression semantic on touched-but-not-rewritten lines."** The original PR #12061 touched the line via `modelProvider → graphProvider` token swap but left the `||` fallback and ternary structure unchanged. Diff-presentation invites focus on changed tokens + away from surrounding expression semantics. Banked as standing checklist item for future review cycles.

Adjacent expressions at lines 128-129 (`consumerContextTokens` / `consumerSafeTokens` hardcoded to `openAiCompatible` knobs) are documented as intentional in the JSDoc comment ("conservative upstream pre-check threshold"); flagging as separate concern under §contributions_over_commits — the "conservative" rationale may not hold across all deployments where Ollama context could be smaller than OpenAiCompatible context. Will file follow-up ticket if peer review confirms the asymmetry isn't deployment-robust; OUT OF SCOPE for this PR.

PR-suite merge unblocked from my side. Ready for operator merge pass.

# PR Review Follow-Up Summary

**Status:** Request Changes

**Cycle:** Cycle 4 — operator-VETO escalation, overriding my prior cycle-3 APPROVED

---

### Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Request Changes
- **Rationale**: Top-level `graphProvider: null` (config.template.mjs:101) + MC-level `graphProvider: AiConfig.graphProvider` (verbatim inherit of null) means the effective deployed value is `null`. Users reading the template see no documented default; users must manually override OR set `NEO_GRAPH_PROVIDER` env var to get any working value. Per operator: *"we have NOTHING. manually change your own config or use an env var, since we have NOTHING. this is NOT how configuration works."* My cycle-2 fix moved the default from `'openAiCompatible'` → `null` to address the client silent-misroute concern, but the operator's veto correctly identifies that the null-default + resolver-derivation pattern is config-anti-pattern (deferred derivation hides the actual behavior from template readers).

*   **PR:** #12061
*   **Target Issue:** #12059
*   **Prior Review Comment ID:** https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/12061#pullrequestreview-4371016921 (cycle-3 APPROVED — corrective re-review for lint compliance)

*   **PR body / close-target changes:** N/A
*   **Branch freshness / merge state:** clean (head still `95ee5536f`)

### Previous Required Actions Audit

For each prior Required Action, mark the current state:

*   **Addressed → REVOKED:** `consumerModel` 3-line ternary collapse — fix landed in cycle-3 commit `95ee5536f` and remains correct; this is NOT the rejection target
*   **NEW BLOCKER from operator-VETO:** `graphProvider: null` at top-level config template + `AiConfig.graphProvider` inherit at MC level both resolve to `null` — config template provides no documented working default
*   **Rejected with rationale:** My cycle-2 framing of "preserve graphProvider null default" was a misframing — the ORIGINAL state had `'openAiCompatible'` as the default; cycle-2 introduced `null` as a NEW state to address the client silent-misroute, but the substitute (resolver-derive) is invisible to template readers. Operator is correct: this is not how configuration should work.

---

### Delta Depth Floor

*   **Delta challenge:** The fundamental tension this PR exposed is unresolved at the shape layer: (a) the client uses ollama for graph, (b) local uses openAiCompatible for graph, (c) any single hardcoded default breaks one of them, (d) null-default + resolver-derive is operator-vetoed as config-anti-pattern. The resolution requires a substrate-shape decision that this PR's current diff cannot deliver alone. Filing as VETO on the current shape; awaiting operator direction on the corrective approach.

    - (A) Concrete default `graphProvider: 'openAiCompatible'` at top-level + JSDoc explicitly documenting `NEO_GRAPH_PROVIDER=ollama` override pattern for the client/ollama deployments. Simple, documented, requires the client operator-side env-var setting (which they already do for OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE etc.)
    - (B) Remove `graphProvider` from configs entirely; `resolveGraphModelProvider()` becomes SSOT, derives from `modelProvider` at consume-time. Override mechanism: explicit param to graph-consuming methods, not config. Loses config-time override.
    - (C) Computed-default pattern: BaseConfig getter computes `graphProvider` from `modelProvider` at config-resolution time. Template shows the computation explicitly (e.g., `graphProvider: () => this.modelProvider === 'ollama' ? 'ollama' : 'openAiCompatible'`). Documented derivation, still overridable per-deployment by setting an explicit value.

*   **Addressed:** "Delete fallback derivation in `resolveGraphModelProvider()`" — evidence: `ai/services/graph/providerDispatch.mjs` reduced from `if (config.graphProvider) return config.graphProvider; return config.modelProvider === 'ollama' ? 'ollama' : 'openAiCompatible';` to `return config.graphProvider;` (single line); JSDoc explicitly states "Configuration is the single source of truth; this helper returns the configured graph provider verbatim instead of synthesizing hidden defaults from other axes"
*   **Addressed:** "Delete `|| 32768` at SemanticGraphExtractor.mjs:128" — evidence: line now reads `const consumerContextTokens = aiConfig.openAiCompatible.contextLimitTokens;` (no fallback)
*   **Addressed:** "Audit rest of PR diff for `||` / hidden-fallback patterns" — evidence: `ai/scripts/runners/runSandman.mjs` audit-pass removed 2 `DEFAULT_*` constants + 7 `|| null` / `|| DEFAULT_*` patterns. The remaining `host ? \`${host.replace(...)}\` : null` ternary at line 60 is defensive code preventing `.replace()` NPE; surfaces missing config as explicit `null` per fail-loud contract (not a hidden default)

### Test-Execution & Location Audit

*   **Changed surface class:** code + test + fixture
*   **Location check:** pass — tests at canonical `test/playwright/unit/ai/`, fixture at `test/playwright/fixtures/`
*   **Related verification run:** CI `unit` check completed SUCCESS at head `33ce62457`. Critical contract-enforcing test in `providerDispatch.spec.mjs` includes negative assertion:
    ```js
    expect(resolveGraphModelProvider({
        modelProvider: 'ollama'
    })).toBeUndefined();
    ```
    This test asserts that when `graphProvider` is unset, resolver returns `undefined` (NOT a fallback like `'ollama'`) — proves the no-fallback contract empirically; would fail under the prior fallback-derivation shape
*   **Findings:** pass — negative-assertion test is the load-bearing contract-enforcement gate. The test rename in `mc-config.template.spec.mjs` from *"preserves null graphProvider defaults so graph routing derives from modelProvider"* → *"inherits concrete graphProvider defaults from Tier-1 config"* additionally documents the contract-aligned shape at the test-prose layer
